technologies.name: React
technologies.purpose: Core UI library for building component-based user interfaces
technologies.rationale: Industry-standard SPA framework with large ecosystem, excellent documentation, strong community support, and team familiarity. Hooks API provides clean state management and side-effects handling.

alternatives.vue: Easier learning curve but smaller enterprise adoption
alternatives.angular: More opinionated but steeper learning curve and more boilerplate
alternatives.svelte: Better performance but smaller ecosystem and less mature

technologies.name: Vite
technologies.purpose: Fast frontend build tool with instant HMR and optimized production builds
technologies.rationale: 10-100x faster than CRA for dev server startup and HMR. Modern ESM-based approach provides near-instant feedback during development. Excellent DX with minimal configuration.

alternatives.create-react-app: Slower builds, less flexible, being deprecated
alternatives.webpack: More complex configuration, slower dev server
alternatives.parcel: Less ecosystem support and plugin availability

technologies.name: React Router
technologies.rationale: De facto standard for React routing. V6 provides cleaner API with hooks, nested routes, and improved data loading patterns. Excellent integration with React 18.

technologies.name: Axios
technologies.purpose: Promise-based HTTP client for API communication
technologies.rationale: Robust HTTP client with interceptors for global error handling, request/response transformation, and automatic JSON parsing. Better API than native fetch for complex scenarios.

alternatives.fetch: Native but lacks interceptors and automatic error handling
alternatives.swr: Includes caching but overkill for simple CRUD

technologies.name: Tailwind CSS
technologies.purpose: Utility-first CSS framework for rapid UI development
technologies.rationale: Utility-first approach enables rapid development without context-switching. Built-in responsive design, dark mode support, and tree-shaking for minimal production CSS. No CSS naming conflicts.

alternatives.bootstrap: Component-based but less flexible, generic look

technologies.name: React Hook Form
technologies.rationale: Minimal re-renders compared to Formik. Built on uncontrolled inputs for better performance. Excellent integration with validation libraries like Zod. Small bundle size (9KB).

technologies.name: Zod
technologies.rationale: Type-safe validation with excellent TypeScript inference. Declarative schema definition with comprehensive error messages. Perfect integration with React Hook Form.

technologies.name: React Testing Library
technologies.purpose: Component testing focused on user behavior
technologies.rationale: Encourages testing from user perspective rather than implementation details. Works seamlessly with Vitest. Industry standard for React testing.

technologies.name: Vitest
technologies.purpose: Fast unit test runner optimized for Vite
technologies.rationale: Native Vite integration with instant HMR for tests. Jest-compatible API but 10x faster. Built-in coverage with c8. Watch mode with smart re-run.

alternatives.jest: Slower, requires additional configuration for ESM
alternatives.mocha: More configuration needed, no built-in assertions

technologies.name: ESLint
technologies.rationale: Industry-standard linter with extensive plugin ecosystem. Catches common errors, enforces code style, and improves code quality.

technologies.name: Prettier
technologies.rationale: Eliminates code style debates. Automatic formatting on save. Works with ESLint via eslint-config-prettier.

technologies.name: Java
technologies.purpose: Backend programming language with long-term support
technologies.rationale: LTS version with modern features (records, sealed classes, pattern matching). Excellent Spring Boot support. Strong typing, extensive tooling, and proven enterprise reliability.

alternatives.java-21: Newer LTS but less ecosystem maturity
alternatives.kotlin: More concise but adds learning curve

technologies.name: Spring Boot
technologies.purpose: Opinionated framework for building production-ready Spring applications
technologies.rationale: Industry-standard for Java enterprise applications. Auto-configuration reduces boilerplate. Comprehensive ecosystem (Data, Security, Cloud). Excellent documentation and community. Built-in production features (metrics, health checks).

alternatives.micronaut: Better for microservices but less mature

technologies.name: Spring Data JPA
technologies.rationale: Eliminates boilerplate CRUD code with repository interfaces. Automatic query generation from method names. Custom query support with @Query. Built-in pagination and sorting.

technologies.name: Hibernate
technologies.purpose: Robust JPA implementation for object-relational mapping
technologies.rationale: De facto standard JPA implementation. Mature, feature-rich, excellent caching support. Handles complex relationships and lazy loading efficiently.

technologies.name: H2 Database
technologies.rationale: Zero configuration setup for development. Fast startup. In-memory mode for testing. File-based mode for persistence. Web console for debugging. Easy migration path to PostgreSQL.

alternatives.sqlite: No server mode, limited SQL features

technologies.productionNote: Migrate to PostgreSQL for production due to scalability and concurrent access limitations

technologies.name: HikariCP
technologies.rationale: Fastest connection pool available. Zero overhead. Spring Boot default. Excellent reliability and monitoring.

technologies.name: Springdoc OpenAPI
technologies.rationale: Automatic API documentation from Spring annotations. Interactive Swagger UI. OpenAPI 3.0 spec generation. Better than Springfox (deprecated).

technologies.name: Lombok
technologies.rationale: Eliminates getter/setter/constructor boilerplate. @Data, @Builder, @Slf4j reduce code by 50%. IDE support excellent. Standard in Spring projects.

alternatives.records: Good for immutable DTOs but can't replace all Lombok features

technologies.name: MapStruct
technologies.purpose: Type-safe bean mapping for Entity ↔ DTO conversion
technologies.rationale: Compile-time code generation (no reflection). Type-safe mapping with compile-time validation. Faster than ModelMapper. Excellent for Entity-DTO conversion.

technologies.name: JUnit 5
technologies.rationale: Modern testing framework with better extension model than JUnit 4. Parameterized tests, nested tests, lifecycle hooks. Spring Boot default.

technologies.name: Mockito
technologies.rationale: De facto standard for Java mocking. Clean API with @Mock and @InjectMocks. Excellent Spring integration. Verification and argument capturing.

technologies.name: MockMvc
technologies.rationale: Test controllers without full server startup. Fast integration tests. JSON response assertions. Security testing support.

technologies.name: AssertJ
technologies.rationale: More readable assertions than JUnit. Fluent API with auto-completion. Better error messages. Strong typing support.

technologies.name: Jackson
technologies.rationale: Spring Boot default JSON processor. Fast, feature-rich. Annotation-based customization. Date/time handling with Java 8 module.

technologies.name: SLF4J + Logback
technologies.rationale: Spring Boot default logging. Powerful configuration. Async logging support. MDC for context. Rolling file appenders.

technologies.name: Docker
technologies.rationale: Ensures consistency between development and production. Isolated environments. Easy dependency management. Industry standard for containerization.

technologies.name: Docker Compose
technologies.rationale: Simple YAML configuration for multi-service applications. Development and production parity. Network isolation. Volume management.

technologies.name: GitHub Actions
technologies.rationale: Native GitHub integration. Free for public repos. YAML-based workflows. Extensive marketplace. Matrix builds for testing.

technologies.name: Maven
technologies.purpose: Java project build and dependency management
technologies.rationale: Spring Boot standard. Central repository. Convention over configuration. Plugin ecosystem. Dependency management.

alternatives.gradle: More flexible but more complex configuration

technologies.name: npm
technologies.rationale: Node.js default package manager. Largest package registry. Lock file for reproducible builds. Scripts for automation.

technologies.name: Git
technologies.rationale: Industry standard version control. Distributed architecture. Branching and merging. GitHub integration.

tools.name: Visual Studio Code
tools.rationale: Excellent React and Java support. Rich extension ecosystem. Integrated terminal and debugging. Free and lightweight.

tools.name: IntelliJ IDEA Community
tools.purpose: Alternative IDE for Spring Boot development
tools.rationale: Superior Java/Spring support. Excellent refactoring. Built-in Maven support. Spring Boot run configurations.

tools.name: Postman
tools.rationale: Industry standard for API testing. Collections for organizing requests. Environment variables. Automated testing.

tools.name: H2 Console
tools.rationale: Built-in web console for database inspection. SQL execution. Schema browsing. Zero configuration.

tools.name: React DevTools
tools.rationale: Essential for React development. Component tree inspection. Props/state debugging. Performance profiling.

summary.licenseCompliance: All technologies use permissive open-source licenses (MIT, Apache 2.0, EPL) suitable for commercial use
summary.productionReadiness: Technology stack is battle-tested and production-ready. H2 database should be migrated to PostgreSQL for production scale.
summary.learningCurve: Moderate - Most technologies have excellent documentation and large communities. Team should be familiar with React and Spring Boot fundamentals.
summary.maintenanceConsiderations: All technologies are actively maintained with regular updates. Spring Boot and React have strong backward compatibility guarantees.
summary.costAnalysis: All core technologies are free and open-source. Only potential costs: Cloud hosting (AWS/Azure/GCP), domain name, SSL certificate (can use Let's Encrypt for free).

versionUpgradeStrategy.frontend: Update React/Vite/dependencies quarterly. Test thoroughly before production deployment. Use npm audit for security updates.
versionUpgradeStrategy.backend: Update Spring Boot on new minor releases (3.2.x → 3.3.x). Stay on Java 17 LTS until 2027. Security patches immediately.
versionUpgradeStrategy.devops: Docker and Docker Compose updates as needed for security. GitHub Actions workflows updated on breaking changes.

database.rationale: Better concurrent access, production reliability, and scalability
database.effort: Low - Spring Data JPA abstracts database specifics

authentication.rationale: User authentication and authorization required for multi-user scenarios
authentication.effort: Medium - Requires backend and frontend changes

stateManagement.rationale: Better performance for complex state or server-state caching
stateManagement.effort: Medium - Requires refactoring of state logic
